volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Pot of Gold (Section 4.10.790 - Section 5.1.832b)

sections.label: Section 4.10.790
sections.original: qui homo culpam admisit in se , nullust tam parvi preti ,
sections.translation: The man that admits a fault is not so much to be despised , if he feels a sense of shame when he excuses himself . Now , Euclio , I do beseech you , that what unawares I have done wrong towards yourself or your daughter , you will grant me pardon for the same , and give her for a wife to me , as the laws demand . I confess that I did violence to your daughter

sections.label: Section 4.10.795
sections.original: Cereris vigiliis , per vinum atque impulsu adulescentiae .
sections.translation: on the festival of Ceres , by reason of wine and the impulse of youth .

sections.label: Section 4.10.796
sections.original: Ei mihi , quód ego facinus ex te aúdio ?
sections.translation: Woe is me ! What shocking deed do I hear of you ?

sections.label: Section 4.10.796b
sections.translation: Why do you exclaim ? You whom I ’ ve made to be a grandfather now at the very wedding of your daughter . For your daughter has just been brought to bed in the ninth month after — calculate the number [*] ; for that reason , in my behalf , has my uncle sent his refusal .

sections.label: Section 4.10.800
sections.original: i intro , exquaere , sitne ita ut ego praedico .
sections.translation: Go in-doors ; enquire whether it is so or not as I say .

sections.label: Section 4.10.800b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone utterly ; so very many misfortunes unite themselves for my undoing . I ’ ll go in-doors , that I may know what of this is true .

sections.label: Section 4.10.802b
sections.translation: I ’ ll follow you this instant . This matter seems now to be pretty nearly in the haven of safety . Now , where to say my servant Strobilus is , I don ’ t know ,

sections.label: Section 4.10.805
sections.original: nisi etiam hic opperiar tamen paulisper ; postea intro
sections.translation: but yet I ’ ll wait here still a little while ; after that I ’ ll follow this man indoors ; now , in the meantime , I ’ ll give him leisure to enquire of the nurse about my doings , the attendant of his daughter , whether she knows the truth .

sections.label: Section 5.1.808
sections.original: Di ímmortales , quíbus et quantis mé donatis gaúdiis .
sections.translation: Immortal Gods , with what and how great delights do you present me ! I ’ ve got a four pound pot filled with gold ; who there is richer than I ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.810
sections.original: quis me Athenis nunc magis quisquam est homo cui di sint propitii ?
sections.translation: What man is there greater than I at Athens now ; any one , I mean , to whom the Gods are propitious ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.811
sections.original: Certo enim ego vocem hic loquentis modo mi audire visus sum .
sections.translation: Why , surely , I seemed just now to hear the voice of some one speaking here .

sections.label: Section 5.1.811b
sections.translation: Ha ! do I not see my master ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.812b
sections.translation: Do I see Strobilus now , my servant ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.813
sections.translation: ’ Tis he himself .

sections.label: Section 5.1.813b
sections.translation: ’ Tis no other .

sections.label: Section 5.1.813c
sections.translation: I ’ ll accost him .

sections.label: Section 5.1.813d
sections.translation: I ’ ll step out [*] towards him . I do think that he has been , as I requested him , to the old woman , the nurse herself of this damsel .

sections.label: Section 5.1.816
sections.original: Quin ego illi me invenisse dico hanc praedam atque eloquar ,
sections.translation: Why don ’ t I tell him that I ’ ve found this prize , and speak out ? For that reason , I ’ ll beg of him to make me free . I ’ ll go and speak to him . ( Addressing him .) I ’ ve found —

sections.label: Section 5.1.818b
sections.translation: What have you found ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.818c
sections.translation: Not that which the boys cry out that they ’ ve found in the bean [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.819b
sections.translation: And are you trifling with me then , as you are in the habit of doing ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.820
sections.translation: Master , stop ; I ’ ll speak out then ; do listen .

sections.label: Section 5.1.820b
sections.translation: Come then , tell me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.820c
sections.translation: I ’ ve found to-day , master very great riches .

sections.label: Section 5.1.821b
sections.translation: Where , pray ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.821c
sections.translation: A four pound pot [*] , I say , full of gold !

sections.label: Section 5.1.822
sections.original: Quod ego facinus audio ex te ? Euclioni hic seni subripuit .
sections.translation: What crime is this that I hear of from you ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.823
sections.translation: Where is this gold ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.823b
sections.original: In arca apud me . nunc volo me emitti manu .
sections.translation: In my box at home ; I now wish to be made free .

sections.label: Section 5.1.824
sections.translation: I , make you free , you fellow ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.825
sections.translation: brimful of wickedness ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.826
sections.original: Abi , ere , scio quam rem geras .
sections.translation: Out upon you , master , I know what you would be at . Troth , I ’ ve cleverly tried your inclination ; you were just getting ready to take it away from me ; what would you do , if I had found it ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.828b
sections.translation: You can ’ t make good your pretences . Come , give up the gold !

sections.label: Section 5.1.829b
sections.translation: I , give up the gold ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.829c
sections.translation: Give it up , I say , that it may be given back to him .

sections.label: Section 5.1.829d
sections.translation: Where am I to get it from ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.830
sections.translation: That which you confessed just now to be in your box .

sections.label: Section 5.1.830b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m in the habit of talking nonsense ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.831b
sections.translation: ’ twas in that way I was speaking .

sections.label: Section 5.1.831c
sections.translation: But do you know what ?—

sections.label: Section 5.1.832b
sections.translation: Even kill me outright , i ’ faith , you never shall get it hence of me ⋯
